subject: Biology
chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production

questions.q: What are the advantages of crop rotation? Explain how it enhances food production.
questions.answer: Crop rotation is the practice of growing different crops on the same land in a sequential manner. The advantages of crop rotation include improved soil fertility, reduced soil erosion, and increased crop yields. It enhances food production by breaking the disease and pest cycle, improving soil structure, and increasing the availability of nutrients for crops.
marking_scheme: Explanation of advantages of crop rotation (2 marks)
Description of how crop rotation enhances food production (3 marks)

questions.q: A farmer harvests 2500 kg of wheat from a field. If the moisture content is 20%, calculate the dry weight of the wheat.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Dry weight = Total weight x (100 - Moisture content)/100
Dry weight = 2500 x (100 - 20)/100
Dry weight = 2500 x 80/100
Dry weight = 2000 kg

questions.q: Describe the role of hybridization in improving crop varieties. Give examples of crops that have been improved through hybridization.
questions.answer: Hybridization is a technique used to improve crop varieties by combining the desirable traits of two different parents. It involves crossing two different varieties of a crop to produce offspring with improved characteristics such as higher yields, disease resistance, or improved quality. Examples of crops that have been improved through hybridization include maize, wheat, and rice.
marking_scheme: Examples of crops improved through hybridization (3 marks)

questions.q: If a farmer uses 200 kg of fertilizer per hectare, and the cost of fertilizer is Rs. 50 per kg, calculate the total cost of fertilizer for a field of 5 hectares.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Total amount of fertilizer used = 200 kg/ha x 5 ha = 1000 kg
Total cost of fertilizer = 1000 kg x Rs. 50/kg = Rs. 50,000
marking_scheme: Correct calculation of total fertilizer used (2 marks)
Accurate calculation of total cost (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of genetic engineering and its application in crop improvement. Give examples of genetically modified crops.
questions.answer: Genetic engineering is a technique used to introduce desirable traits into crops by manipulating their genes. It involves the use of biotechnology to insert genes from one species into the DNA of another species. Genetic engineering has been used to develop crops with improved traits such as pest resistance, drought tolerance, and increased nutritional content. Examples of genetically modified crops include Bt cotton, golden rice, and pest-resistant maize.
marking_scheme: Explanation of genetic engineering concept (2 marks)
Examples of genetically modified crops (3 marks)

questions.q: A farmer has 1000 kg of seeds with a germination rate of 80%. If the seeds are sown in a field with an area of 10 hectares, calculate the number of seeds required per hectare to achieve a plant density of 200 plants per hectare.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Number of seeds that germinate = 1000 kg x 80% = 800 kg
Number of seeds required per hectare = 200 plants/ha / (800 kg / 1000 kg) = 250 seeds/ha
marking_scheme: Correct calculation of germinated seeds (2 marks)
Accurate calculation of seeds required per hectare (3 marks)
